title: hieracium naegelianum subsp. andreae
intro: Hieracium naegelianum subsp. andreae, also known as Hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the dandelion family and is native to certain parts of Europe. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of inflammation and pain, due to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Hieracium naegelianum subsp. andreae include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying the leaves and flowers, and then infusing them in hot water to make a tea, or using the plant in tincture or capsule form to harness its medicinal properties.
